raw_text:
All variance components are now fully corroborated. The $18,500 Contractor Spend overage is fully accounted for: ~$12,000 from TechForce (DevOps) and NovaBuild (Backend) plus $6,500 for an AWS migration specialist extension. I now have sufficient evidence to produce the full commentary.

---

# VARIANCE COMMENTARY — November 2024

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

November 2024 saw broad salary overspend across all five departmental salary lines (+$49,400 in aggregate) and a near-doubling of Contractor Spend (+$18,500, +92.5%), driven by a convergence of distinct but largely explainable events. Engineering and Product overruns reflect timing anomalies — an early-start new hire and a same-month overlap of an outgoing and incoming employee — rather than structural cost growth. Operations reflects a planned (though unbudgeted) peak-season headcount build. Marketing's overage is a misclassified bonus payment that correctly belongs on a separate bonus line. G&A reflects a finance contractor posted to the wrong cost category. Contractor Spend is fully explained by a CTO-approved platform migration engagement across three contractors, including a mid-month specialist extension. With the exception of Marketing Salaries (reclassification recommended) and G&A Salaries (coding correction recommended), the majority of variances are either one-time in nature or will self-correct in December. Forward budgets should be reviewed for Operations (peak season runs through December) and the continuing G&A contractor engagement.

---

## LINE COMMENTARY

---

**Engineering Salaries | +$18,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $120,000 | Actual: $138,000**

The $18,000 overspend is driven by the first full payroll cycle for Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5), who onboarded on 28 October — approximately one week ahead of her originally planned 4 November start date. Her base compensation of $185,000 annualised meant her first full monthly salary charge hit the November payroll run in its entirety, while the budget had modelled a partial-month cost for November. The early start was at the employee's request and was accommodated operationally, but the budget was not adjusted to reflect the revised timing. This variance is a one-time timing effect; December should normalise to budget assuming no further headcount changes.

- Sources:
  - Gmail — 2024-11-20T18:15:00Z — gmail-engineering_salaries-broad — "Offer letter to Priya Sharma — start date 28 October per her request. Original plan was 4 November. First full payroll: November."
  - Slack — 2024-11-20T12:15:00Z — slack-engineering_salaries-broad — "Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5) started 28 October — first full salary run hits November. Comp: $185K base. Previous plan assumed November start but she onboarded a week early, creating one extra pay period overlap."

---

**Product Salaries | +$12,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $92,000**

The $12,000 overspend results from a one-month double-salary overlap in the Product team. Marcus (outgoing PM) continued to serve his notice period through 30 November, while his replacement Sarah commenced in the role on 1 November. Both individuals were therefore on payroll for the full month. Marcus is expected to exit at month-end, and this overlap cost will not recur in December. The budget modelled only one salary for this role in November, as the transition timing was not reflected in the original headcount plan.

- Sources:
  - Slack — 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z — slack-product_salaries-broad — "Marcus (outgoing PM) is serving his notice through 30 November. Sarah (incoming PM) started 1 November. Double salary in November only — Marcus exits end of month."

---

**Marketing Salaries | +$8,400 (+14.0%) | Budget: $60,000 | Actual: $68,400**

The $8,400 overspend matches exactly a Q4 performance bonus payment of $8,400 distributed to the Marketing team on 15 November, based on approved Q3 performance review outcomes. Critically, this payment has been posted to the Marketing Salaries cost line rather than a dedicated Bonuses line. The underlying base salary for Marketing is therefore on budget; the variance is entirely a coding/classification issue. **A journal reclassification to the Bonuses line is recommended.** Finance should also review whether the Bonuses budget line has capacity to absorb this charge or whether a budget transfer is required.

- Sources:
  - Slack — 2024-11-18T12:15:00Z — slack-marketing_salaries-broad — "Q4 performance bonuses paid 15 November for Marketing team. $8,400 total across the team per the approved Q3 performance review outcomes. Separate from base salary line — should have been budgeted under bonuses but posted to salaries."

---

**Operations Salaries | +$7,000 (+17.5%) | Budget: $40,000 | Actual: $47,000**

The $7,000 overspend is attributable to five temporary warehouse staff engaged via an agency for peak season coverage, commencing 1 November. The additional cost of $7,000 per month was discussed in an Operations budget review and is expected to continue through December. However, this planned seasonal ramp was not incorporated into the original November (or December) budget. The cost has been posted to Operations Salaries rather than a separate temporary labour or agency line, which may warrant a coding review. **December budget should be updated to reflect the ongoing $7,000 monthly exposure for the remainder of the peak season.**

- Sources:
  - Slack — 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z — slack-operations_salaries-broad — "Peak season starts — 5 temporary warehouse staff added for November and December. Bringing in via agency, posting to Ops Salaries. Cost: $7,000 additional per month. Planned in operations budget review, not reflected in original headcount plan."

---

**G&A Salaries | +$4,000 (+13.3%) | Budget: $30,000 | Actual: $34,000**

The $4,000 overspend reflects the engagement of Clara Mendez, a finance contractor brought in for Q4 year-end close support at $4,000 per month on a three-month engagement. Per CFO instruction, this cost has been posted to G&A Salaries. As a contractor rather than a permanent employee, this spend would typically be classified under Contractor Spend or a professional services line rather than Salaries. **A reclassification should be considered to maintain clean separation between employee compensation and third-party contractor costs.** The engagement is expected to continue through Q1 2025, so the forward budget should be updated accordingly.

- Sources:
  - Slack — 2024-11-12T12:15:00Z — slack-g&a_salaries-broad — "Brought in Clara Mendez (finance contractor) for Q4 close support. $4,000/month, posting to G&A Salaries per CFO instruction. Year-end close support, 3-month engagement."

---

**Contractor Spend | +$18,500 (+92.5%) | Budget: $20,000 | Actual: $38,500**

The $18,500 overspend is fully explained by a CTO-approved platform migration programme involving three external contractors, with costs accumulating across the full month:

- **TechForce Ltd (DevOps) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend):** Both engaged under a formally co-signed SOW (CTO and CFO) on an 8-week engagement commencing 28 October, at day rates of $750 and $700 respectively. Estimated November cost for these two contractors was approximately $12,000.
- **AWS Migration Specialist (Amendment 1):** An additional specialist was engaged mid-month for a 2-week extension at a total cost of $6,500, approved by the CTO and appended to the existing SOW as Amendment 1.

The combined $18,500 increment over budget ($12,000 + $6,500) accounts for the full variance. The budget did not anticipate the scale of this engagement. The 8-week SOW extends into December; further contractor costs should be expected on this line and the December budget updated. **Note:** if the Clara Mendez finance contractor cost (captured under G&A Salaries above) is reclassified, Contractor Spend actual would increase further to $42,500.

- Sources:
  - Gmail — 2024-11-16T18:15:00Z — gmail-contractor_spend-broad — "SOW executed with TechForce Ltd (DevOps contractor) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend contractor). 8-week engagement from 28 Oct. Day rate schedule attached. CTO and CFO co-signed."
  - Slack — 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z — slack-contractor_spend-broad — "Platform migration kicked off — two senior contractors (DevOps + Backend) engaged for 8 weeks from 28 Oct. Day rates $750 and $700 per day respectively. Estimated Nov cost ~$12,000. CTO approved."
  - Gmail — 2024-11-16T18:45:00Z — gmail-contractor_spend-narrow — "CTO email — AWS migration specialist engaged for 2 additional weeks. $6,500 total. Approved. Added to existing SOW as Amendment 1."
  - Slack — 2024-11-16T12:45:00Z — slack-contractor_spend-narrow — "Additional $6,500 for AWS migration specialist added week 3 — CTO approved extension."

---

## INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES

The following lines showed variances below the significance threshold and were not investigated with research tools. No commentary action is required, though they should be monitored given the broader salary overspend environment.

- **Sales Salaries** — $6,000 overspend (+4.0%). Within normal tolerance; likely reflects minor timing or commission-adjacent adjustments.
- **Benefits & Payroll Taxes** — $4,000 overspend (+8.0%). Directionally consistent with the aggregate salary overruns noted across all departments this month, as benefits and payroll taxes scale with gross payroll. Expected to partially self-correct in December as one-time salary items unwind.
